region_of_origin: Central Asia (Cannabis sativa hemp native origin); major commercial cultivation in Canada and Germany
food_subgroup: Seeds — hulled hemp seeds (hemp hearts); complete protein source providing all 9 essential amino acids

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for hulled hemp seeds. Closest parent concept: Cannabis sativa seed food product. FoodOn URI left null pending formal ontology entry.
ontology.foodex2_description: Hulled hemp seed kernels (Cannabis sativa, hemp variety)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0SHM used as closest parent match for hulled hemp seed kernels; no exact FoodEx2 term for hemp hearts specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for hulled hemp seeds as a food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard clinical serving of hulled hemp seeds is approximately 3 tablespoons (30g), providing about 166 kcal, 9.5g complete protein, 14.6g fat, and 210mg magnesium (approximately 50% of the adult male RDI).

nutrient_source.title: Seeds, hemp seed, hulled — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central entry for hulled hemp seeds (Cannabis sativa, seeds, hemp seed, hulled). Values cross-referenced with Health Canada hemp food composition data. All core macros and micronutrients confirmed from USDA FDC as primary approved source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of hemp seeds estimated from minimal available digestible carbohydrate content; analogous low-GI seed reference from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for hulled hemp seeds exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 15 based on: (1) total carbohydrate 8.7g per 100g of which 4g is dietary fibre, yielding approximately 4.7g available carbohydrate per 100g; (2) analogy with other low-carbohydrate seeds (flaxseed GI ~35, chia seed GI ~1) from University of Sydney GI Database; (3) predominantly fat and protein macronutrient profile which blunts glycemic response. GL per 30g serving = (15 × 4.7 × 0.30) / 100 ≈ 0.4. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (5mg/100g), high potassium (859mg/100g), high magnesium (700mg/100g), and arginine content supporting nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation
hypertension.guidance: Hulled hemp seeds are highly suitable for hypertension management: sodium content is negligible at 5mg per 100g, potassium at 859mg and magnesium at 700mg per 100g provide clinically meaningful antihypertensive mineral support, and arginine (~10g/100g) serves as a nitric oxide precursor promoting endothelial vasodilation and blood pressure reduction.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~15), negligible glycemic load, exceptional magnesium density (700mg/100g) improving insulin sensitivity, and high complete protein promoting satiety
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hulled hemp seeds are well-suited for Type 2 Diabetes: GI of approximately 15 with a glycemic load of 0.4 per 30g serving produces negligible postprandial glucose response; magnesium at 700mg per 100g (approximately 210mg per 3-tablespoon serving, ~50% male RDI) directly addresses the magnesium deficiency prevalent in T2DM that contributes to insulin resistance, and 9.5g complete protein per 30g serving supports satiety and glycemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Optimal omega-6:omega-3 ratio of 3:1 (36.2g PUFA per 100g), GLA (~1.8g/100g) with anti-inflammatory properties, zero cholesterol, and low saturated fat (5.2g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hulled hemp seeds provide a cardioprotective lipid profile: 36.2g PUFA per 100g with an optimal omega-6:omega-3 ratio of approximately 3:1 supports reduction of systemic inflammation and LDL cholesterol; gamma-linolenic acid (GLA ~1.8g/100g) contributes to anti-inflammatory prostaglandin E1 production; cholesterol is zero and saturated fat is low at 5.2g/100g, making hemp hearts a heart-healthy addition to a cardiovascular disease prevention diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: GLA (~540mg per 30g serving) contributing to anti-inflammatory PGE1 production; optimal omega-6:omega-3 ratio (3:1); high zinc (~2.97mg per 30g serving)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Hemp hearts provide meaningful anti-inflammatory support for rheumatoid arthritis through GLA (~540mg per 3-tablespoon serving contributing to PGE1 anti-inflammatory pathway), an optimal omega-6:omega-3 ratio of 3:1 that actively improves the systemic inflammatory omega balance, and zinc (~2.97mg per 30g serving) relevant for RA immune modulation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hulled hemp seeds (hemp hearts) are the shelled inner kernels of Cannabis sativa (hemp variety), minimally processed by removing the outer hull to yield soft, cream-coloured seeds with a mild, nutty flavour and slightly chewy texture. They are legally sold in Singapore as a food product (Cannabis sativa seeds with less than 0.2% THC are classified as food, not controlled substances). Per 100g, hemp hearts provide 553 kcal, 31.6g of complete protein containing all 9 essential amino acids (including edestin and albumin, highly bioavailable plant proteins), 48.8g fat (36.2g PUFA with an optimal omega-6:omega-3 ratio of approximately 3:1, and gamma-linolenic acid GLA ~1.8g with anti-inflammatory properties), 8.7g carbohydrate, and 4g dietary fibre. Sodium is negligible at 5mg per 100g. The glycemic index is approximately 15 (low) with a glycemic load of 0.4 per standard 30g serving. Hemp hearts are exceptionally rich in magnesium at approximately 700mg per 100g — among the highest magnesium density of any commonly available food — making them clinically relevant for insulin resistance, hypertension, and cardiovascular health. A standard clinical serving is 3 tablespoons (~30g), providing approximately 166 kcal, 9.5g complete protein, 14.6g fat, and 210mg magnesium (~50% adult male RDI). Hemp hearts are vegan, vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Available at Cold Storage, specialty health food stores, and online retailers in Singapore.
common_user_queries: where to buy hemp seeds Singapore

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central (exact match: seeds, hemp seed, hulled), an approved primary source. All values are consistent with published hemp seed composition literature. GI is an analogous estimate (no direct measurement available) with confidence 0.9 based on minimal available carbohydrate and analogy with low-GI seeds from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: water_g set to -1: detailed moisture content for hulled hemp seeds not separately reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry consulted; set to -1 as data not available. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — hemp seeds contain no retinol or beta-carotene. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC — plant food with no B12. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC — plant food. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC. GI value of 15 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for hulled hemp seeds exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the access date. Estimation basis: 4.7g available carbohydrate per 100g (8.7g total minus 4g fibre), analogy with low-carbohydrate seeds (flaxseed GI ~35, chia GI ~1), and predominantly fat-protein macronutrient profile. FoodOn URI is null: no exact FoodOn term for hulled hemp seeds exists; closest parent noted. FoodEx2 code A0SHM used as closest parent match. SNOMED CT code is null: no applicable clinical food code identified. Legal status in Singapore: Cannabis sativa seeds with less than 0.2% THC are classified as food products and are legally sold. monounsaturated_fat_g value of 6.0g is an approximation derived from total fat (48.8g) minus saturated fat (5.2g) minus polyunsaturated fat (36.2g) minus trans fat (0.0g) = approximately 7.4g; USDA FDC reports MUFA for hemp seeds at approximately 6.0g; value used is from USDA FDC. tamil and hindi local_names are null: hemp seeds are not a traditional Tamil or Hindi-named food item with a standardised local-language name; English and Chinese names are the primary retrieval forms in Singapore context.
